calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Albert Lee appeared by zoom for and with John Reed.

The court noted that there is no appearance by the petitioner and that Mr. Lee has provided objections to venue. Mr. Lee stated that he received an email stating that the parties are amenable to stipulating to change of venue. The court sustains the objections as to venue. The Petition for Letters of Administration is dismissed without prejudice so that the petition can be filed in the proper venue.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-30-2023 Continued To Jul-11-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sarah Cullen appeared by zoom for and with Yvette Reed by zoom, Todd Reed by zoom and Michelle Reed zoom.
Ablert Lee for and with John Reed (objector/son).

Mr. Lee stated that his client is objecting to venue and jurisdiction, that his client believes this was filed in the wrong County and that there are no assets to be probated. Mrs. Cullen stated that the issue of the assets seems to be the main point of contention, that the objector alleges that his mother executed a deed granting him joint tenancy, but the deed was never recorded, and that her clients believe the deed is not valid. Mr. Lee stated that he filed his objections, but it got rejected, that he can re-file by the end of next week and that he has a certified copy of the death certificate showing that the decedent died in Alameda County. The court instructed Mr. Lee to file the death certificate. In light of the objections, the Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to July 11, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Lee is to file his objections by June 13, 2023. Mrs. Cullen is to file her response by June 27, 2023.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Probate Examiner: Aeyoung Kim. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
